title: v3.0.0 Stabilization
Backfill request_uuid for non-HTTP audit creation paths
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by audit-compliance agent review (Q2).**

The audited gem auto-populates `request_uuid` only inside Rails HTTP requests (via `Audited::Sweeper` Rack middleware). Verified: 3492/5126 (68%) of dev DB audits have NULL request_uuid — all `auditable_type='BaseRule'` from seed/import paths.

## Affected paths

- Rake tasks (e.g. `stig_and_srg_puller:pull`)
- Seeds (`db/seeds.rb`)
- ActiveJob workers
- `Component#duplicate_reviews_and_history` (Ruby method, not request-scoped)
- `ReviewBuilder` import path (uses `Review.insert!` so no audit at all)
- `after_commit` / async hooks dispatched after request ends

## Impact

`AuditEventBundle.bundled_with` (commit `7a7fc2e`) returns just the trigger row when request_uuid is nil — forensic correlation breaks for any non-HTTP-driven multi-row operation.

## Fix shape

In `app/lib/vulcan_audit.rb`, add `before_create :backfill_request_uuid_for_jobs` that pulls from `Audited.store` OR a thread-local set by job middleware (e.g. `ActiveJob::Base.around_perform`).

Document the boundary in `post-merge-remediation-notes.md`: "request_uuid correlation requires either an HTTP request or job middleware that sets the thread-local."

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Job middleware sets `Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]` per job
- [ ] Rake-task helper sets it for long-running tasks
- [ ] before_create hook backfills if unset (uses SecureRandom.uuid for orphans, with a flag column or comment indicating "non-request-scoped")
- [ ] Test: audit created in a job has request_uuid
- [ ] Test: audit created in a rake task has request_uuid (with helper)
notes:
Surfaced by audit-compliance agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Closes Q2 forensic correlation gap.
[2026-05-02] Sequencing agent flagged the .4 → 14r edge as false serialization — AuditEventBundle (the .4 component 14r consumes) already shipped in commit 7a7fc2e. 14r is technically parallel-safe with remaining .4 work (F1/F2/F3/F5/F6/F7 don't touch app/lib/vulcan_audit.rb). bd dep remove not available in this bd version; edge remains as a soft block until .4 closes.

title: move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`reviews_controller.rb:629-635` `move_review_subtree!` writes one audit per moved review (good), and via `vulcan_audited associated_with: :rule` (`review.rb:48`) the audit is attached to the NEW rule. Source rule has no record of an outbound move.

## Forensic asymmetry

- Reviewer auditing destination rule Z: sees "comment Y moved here" ✓
- Reviewer auditing source rule X: sees nothing about Y leaving ✗

## Fix

Before walking subtree, write `action: 'review_moved_out'` audit on source rule referencing destination rule_id + review_id. Mirror the pattern at `reviews_controller.rb:398` (Component-level audit before destroy).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule before walk
- [ ] Audit row carries: source_rule_id, destination_rule_id, review_id, audit_comment
- [ ] Test: source rule's audit feed shows the outbound entry
- [ ] Test: destination rule's audit feed still shows the inbound (existing behavior unchanged)
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Forensic completeness for cross-rule operations.
close_reason: Outbound audit on source rule shipped. 4 new specs, 98/98 reviews_spec green.

title: Two-pass validate_foreign_key for original lifecycle migration FKs
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`db/migrate/20260429145530_add_lifecycle_columns_to_reviews.rb:24-27` adds 4 FKs (`triage_set_by_id`, `adjudicated_by_id`, `duplicate_of_review_id`, `responding_to_review_id`) inline with column adds. Rails 8 default `add_foreign_key` validates immediately, taking ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the `reviews` table for the duration of validation. On a populated production reviews table this can lock writes for seconds.

Same anti-pattern as the pre-fix index migration (`.2`) — same Strong Migrations 2-pass remediation.

## Fix

Convert to two-pass per Strong Migrations:
1. Original migration: change to `add_foreign_key … validate: false`
2. New migration: `disable_ddl_transaction!` + `validate_foreign_key :reviews, column: ...` for each of the 4 FKs

Already past initial deploy on `feat/viewer-comments` so this is post-merge cleanup, not blocker.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 4 FKs have `validate: false` on `add_foreign_key`
- [ ] New `disable_ddl_transaction!` migration runs `validate_foreign_key` for each
- [ ] Schema.rb identical net state
- [ ] Verified on fresh test DB
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Post-merge cleanup; not blocker.

title: Add zip-bomb decompression budget to json_archive import
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by security-review agent on `.4` work.** `JsonArchiveImporter` accepts operator-uploaded zip archives. Current controls:

- `100.megabytes` upload cap (`projects_controller.rb:20`) — **pre-decompression only**
- rubyzip 2.4.x `Zip.validate_entry_sizes` defaults true — **per-entry only, not aggregate**
- Whole import wrapped in one `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction` (`json_archive_importer.rb:179`) — DB connection held for entire decompression duration

## Threat

A 50–100 MB JSON-of-empty-arrays archive decompresses to multiple GB before Ruby OOMs. Even non-malicious operator misuse (fat-fingered export of a huge component history) hangs the worker + holds the DB connection.

Trusted-admin model means severity is "noisy worker hang" not "data corruption", but the failure mode is opaque and happens silently until OOM.

## Fix shape

- Iterate archive entries computing `entry.size` (uncompressed) against an aggregate budget (proposed: 500 MB)
- Reject with HTTP 413 + clear toast before parsing any entry
- Add unit spec with a fixture archive that exceeds budget

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `JsonArchiveImporter` enumerates entries, computes total uncompressed size before reading
- [ ] Reject with HTTP 413 + clear error message when over budget
- [ ] Per-archive budget configurable via Settings (default 500 MB)
- [ ] Test: archive exceeding budget rejected before DB transaction opens
- [ ] Test: archive under budget proceeds normally
- [ ] No regression on existing 47 importer specs
notes: Surfaced by security agent review on .4, 2026-05-02. M-effort. Trusted-admin upload context limits severity, but failure mode is opaque/silent.
close_reason: Decompression budget shipped via Settings.import.json_archive_size_budget_mb (default 500 MB). 3 new specs, 51/51 importer specs green.

title: Expand ReviewBlueprint default fields (eliminate frontend refetch)
description:
**Updated 2026-05-02 with cross-validation from Rails-architect + design-review agents on `.4` work.**

`app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb` default fields = `id, action, comment, created_at, name, triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at, triager_display_name, triager_imported, adjudicator_display_name, adjudicator_imported` (after `.8` work in commit `fafb71b`).

**Still missing for full frontend self-sufficiency** (per Rails-architect agent Lens 8):
- `rule_id` (CommentTriageModal needs to read it for picker scope)
- `section` (modal renders SectionLabel)
- `responding_to_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a reply)
- `duplicate_of_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a duplicate)
- `triage_set_by_id` (forensic queries; today only `triage_set_by_imported_email/name` are exposed for the imported case)
- `author_name` (modal renders blockquote header)
- `author_email` (gated — only when caller is admin tier; mirrors disposition export pattern)

## Why it matters

When triage/adjudicate/section/admin endpoints (`reviews_controller.rb` 8 sites) return `ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash(@review)`, the modal cannot refresh in place — it must refetch the parent table row via `this.fetch()`. Net: every mutation = 2 round trips.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default fields expanded to include the 7 missing lifecycle fields
- [ ] author_email gated by `options[:include_email]` in the blueprint (mirror disposition export pattern)
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap (vulcan-component-blueprint-default-fields-trap memory): ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] CommentTriageModal can update its own state from the response payload alone (no `this.fetch()` after triage/adjudicate)
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes all 7 fields
- [ ] Test: author_email present only when include_email: true option passed
- [ ] Test: existing CommentTriageModal vitest specs pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Manual verification: triage a comment via UI, confirm no follow-up `/components/:id/comments` request fires (DevTools Network tab)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Default fields expanded to include all PR-717 lifecycle fields
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap: ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes triage_status, section, etc.
- [ ] Test: existing ComponentComments tests pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Frontend can drop the post-event refetch (optional follow-up)
notes:
[2026-05-02] Cross-validated with Rails-architect agent on .4 review. Confirmed silent-incomplete payload forces frontend refetch. Promoted P2→P1.
[2026-05-02] CLOSED — 3 commits on feat/viewer-comments. Both ACs met.

Commits:
  7cb0990  expand ReviewBlueprint default fields (primary deliverable)
  da06857  flatten author_email describe to fit RSpec/NestedGroups limit
  6eece58  refresh row in place after triage/adjudicate (no refetch — frontend follow-up)

Implementation:

Phase 1 — Blueprint expansion (7cb0990):
- Added 6 fields to default: rule_id, section, responding_to_review_id, duplicate_of_review_id, triage_set_by_id, author_name
- Added author_email as conditional field gated by render_as_hash(review, include_email: true) — mirrors disposition_matrix_export include_email pattern
- user_id stays excluded as a public-comment correlation guard (intentional asymmetry — admin-tier triage_set_by_id IS exposed for forensic queries; viewer-tier user_id is NOT to prevent cross-comment author correlation during open windows)
- 10 specs added covering field presence + author_email gating

Phase 2 — Frontend refresh-in-place (6eece58):
- ComponentComments.onTriaged / onAdjudicated now call updateRowInPlace(payload) instead of this.fetch()
- updateRowInPlace finds the matching row by id and merges the response payload over the existing row (preserves rule_displayed_name which is computed in paginated_comments, not in the blueprint)
- Defensive fallback to fetch() when payload missing or row not in current page
- 4 vitest specs added covering in-place update + preservation + fallback

ACs all met:
- [x] Default fields expanded to include the 7 missing lifecycle fields
- [x] author_email gated by include_email option
- [x] ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap N/A — no Review.select(...) queries exist anywhere
- [x] CommentTriageModal can update its own state from the response payload alone (the modal already had everything it needs from the modal-side this.review prop; the gap was on the parent ComponentComments side, fixed in 6eece58)
- [x] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes all 7 fields
- [x] Test: author_email present only when include_email: true
- [x] Test: existing CommentTriageModal vitest specs pass with the richer payload (40/40)
- [-] Manual UI verification: not yet done — require running the dev server and DevTools Network tab. Vitest covers the no-fetch behavior at the unit level. (Calling out this gap explicitly per project rule "if you can't test the UI, say so.")

Test results:
- 2255/2255 backend specs green (rake spec:parallel, 3:56)
- 2289/2289 vitest specs green (yarn vitest run, 21s)

Now-unblocked: vulcan-v3.x-1dj.39 (P3 — create endpoint return review payload to eliminate post-create refetch — same pattern as this card, scope is the public comment POST flow).
[2026-05-02 manual UI verification] AC checked off via Playwright on the running dev server.

Flow:
- Logged in admin@example.com → /components/8/triage
- Pending comment row #1 (CNTR-01-000001 Check) had "Triage" action button
- Opened modal: byline rendered "Lyda Lang · posted 4/29/2026..." (commenter_display_name path resolved to user.name; no imported badge — correct, user is live on this instance)
- Selected "Accept (Concur)" radio → Save decision
- Network log captured for full session: 1 GET /components/8/comments (page load) + 1 PATCH /reviews/1/triage (save) + ZERO post-save GETs on /components/8/comments
- Row #1 transitioned in place from "Pending Triage / Triage" to "Accept / Edit / Close" — full row state refreshed from the PATCH response payload alone

Pre-.20 baseline would have produced 2 GETs to /components/8/comments (load + post-save refetch via this.fetch()). Confirming the .20 deliverable: 2 round trips → 1.

Final AC checkbox: [x] Manual verification: triage a comment via UI, confirm no follow-up `/components/:id/comments` request fires (DevTools Network tab) — DONE.
close_reason: Blueprint expansion + frontend refresh-in-place shipped. 2255/2255 backend, 2289/2289 vitest.

title: Strengthen section-edit save test: assert form-state cleanup post-save
description: `spec/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.spec.js:471-493` asserts `hideSpy` was called with modal id, but doesn't verify form-state cleanup. Implementation calls `cancelSectionEdit()` BEFORE `$bvModal.hide(...)` (CommentTriageModal.vue:487-494). A regression that flips the order or skips `cancelSectionEdit()` would still pass this test.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test asserts `expect(w.vm.sectionEditMode).toBe(false)` after save
- [ ] Test asserts `expect(w.vm.sectionAuditComment).toBe("")` after save
- [ ] Test asserts `expect(w.vm.newSection).toBe(null)` after save
- [ ] Spec passes (vitest)

title: Strengthen move_to_rule test: 3-level reply chain to catch single-depth bugs
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb:1070-1076` test for move_to_rule reply recursion only verifies depth=1. The recursive `move_review_subtree!` at `reviews_controller.rb:618-624` walks N-deep. A bug like `responses.first&.update!(rule_id: ...)` or "only descend one level" would pass the test.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Add `nested_reply` (reply-of-reply) to `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb#PATCH /reviews/:id/move_to_rule` setup
- [ ] Assert `nested_reply.reload.rule_id == rule_b.id` after one move_to_rule call
- [ ] Spec passes
close_reason: Strengthened in 3-level reply chain test — would now catch single-depth regression in move_review_subtree!. RuboCop clean.

title: Strengthen idempotent section test: target the controller short-circuit explicitly
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb:1196-1203` idempotent test trivially passes. Pre-condition `update!(section: 'check_content')` runs WITHOUT `audit_comment` set, so audited gem records 1 audit. The PATCH `section: 'check_content', audit_comment: 'noop'` is a no-op. The assertion `audits.count == before_count` would pass even if the controller wrote an audit when input matches current — Rails `update!(same_value)` triggers no Dirty change, no audit. Test catches nothing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test rewritten to assert controller short-circuited (e.g., response body contains `{idempotent: true}` if added, or assertion that the audit_comment string is NOT in any audit) — proves the test catches a regression where the controller writes a redundant audit
- [ ] Spec passes
close_reason: Idempotent flag surfaced in response. Spec asserts presence on no-change + absence on real change. RuboCop clean, 10/10 section specs green.

title: Prevent audit-laundering chain (admin_destroy → re-import wipes trail)
description: Combines H2 + H4. After `admin_destroy` (`reviews_controller.rb:373-406`) cascades replies, audited gem keeps audit rows for destroyed records (good). But re-import via `Review.insert!` skips audited entirely — re-imported review has no audit record. Malicious admin can destroy + re-import to launder lifecycle history (no triage_set_by audit, no destroy event tied to resurrected row).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On import, write a Component-level audit record listing imported review external_ids with source archive identifier
- [ ] Test: import a fresh archive — Component has audit row `action='import_reviews'` with external_ids list
- [ ] Test: destroy review, export, import — destroyed-then-restored review's history is reconstructible from Component audit
- [ ] Documented in `docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/` followup notes
notes:
[2026-05-01 closed in commit 2db6507]
- ReviewBuilder writes Component-level audit row per import: action='import_reviews', audited_changes={archive_vulcan_version, archive_exported_at, review_external_ids}, comment="Imported N reviews from backup archive (vulcan_version=X, exported_at=Y)".
- Audit row only created when ReviewBuilder receives both component: and manifest: kwargs (test/legacy callers without those kwargs skip the audit).
- JsonArchiveImporter accepts imported_by: kwarg, threads it + manifest into ReviewBuilder.
- Tests: 5 unit specs on ReviewBuilder.write_import_audit + 1 integration test via full importer flow.
- Followup notes: docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/post-merge-remediation-notes.md (covers .2, .9, .10).
- Reconstruction: union of (a) per-Review destroy audits on originating instance + (b) Component import_reviews row → traces destroyed-then-restored review back to source archive.
- Acceptance: all 4 ACs met (audit row written, external_ids list, source archive identified, documented).
close_reason: Component-level import audit committed in 2db6507. 12/22 cards closed on epic.

title: json_archive: validate Review records during import (insert! bypasses validators)
description: `app/services/import/json_archive/review_builder.rb:69-73` uses `Review.insert!` which skips ALL new validators (duplicate_status_requires_target, no_self_responding_reference, responding_to_must_be_same_rule, duplicate_of_must_be_same_component, duplicate_of_must_not_be_a_duplicate, inclusion validators on triage_status + section). Malicious or legacy archive can re-introduce data the validators were added to prevent.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Post-insert validation pass: re-load each inserted Review and call `valid?`
- [ ] On invalid: warning logged with review external_id + validation failure messages, Review marked or removed per Aaron's call
- [ ] Test: archive containing a duplicate-marked review with no target — import warns, does not corrupt DB
- [ ] Test: archive containing a reply with mismatched rule — import warns
- [ ] Test: clean archive imports without warnings
notes:
[2026-05-01 closed in commit bf6a34d]
- ReviewBuilder#build_all: post-insert validation pass via review.valid?(:import_integrity).
- Invalid records: warning emitted (with external_id + full validator messages), row deleted to keep DB clean.
- Children point at removed parents → FK on_delete: :cascade handles.
- Review model: user-action validators (validate_project_permissions, can_request_review, can_revoke_review_request, can_request_changes, can_approve, can_lock_control, can_unlock_control) tagged on: %i[create update] — Rails Guides §7.3 canonical pattern. Behavior on normal saves identical (AR uses :create/:update implicit context); :import_integrity context runs only data-integrity validators.
- Tests: 4 ReviewBuilder unit specs + verified 47 importer specs, 82 model specs, 87 reviews requests, full 1771-spec parallel run all GREEN.
- Aaron caught my initial attr_accessor :skip_role_validations as a hack; researched Rails Guides §7.3 (https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_validations.html) and switched to canonical custom validation contexts.

title: Add associated_with: :rule to vulcan_audited on Review (audit-trail recoverability)
description: `app/models/review.rb:43` `vulcan_audited only:` lacks `associated_with: :rule`. All other audited models use it (Rule, Membership, AdditionalQuestion, etc.). After `admin_destroy` cascade, audit rows are orphaned — `auditable_id` points to a deleted Review, no `associated_id` to query through. Federal compliance posture: trail exists but is queryable only via raw SQL. Comment at `reviews_controller.rb:372` acknowledges the gap.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `vulcan_audited only: %i[...], associated_with: :rule` on Review model
- [ ] Backfill migration: existing Review audits get `associated_id`/`associated_type` populated
- [ ] Test: `Audited::Audit.where(associated_type: 'Rule', associated_id: rule.id)` returns the review's audit history
- [ ] Test: post-admin_destroy, audit rows still queryable through Rule association
- [ ] Comment at `reviews_controller.rb:372` updated to reflect new mechanism
close_reason: associated_with :rule added to Review's vulcan_audited. Backfill migration populates legacy audits. STI gotcha: associated_type stores 'BaseRule' (polymorphic-STI). 155/155 reviews regression green.

title: json_archive: warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by
description:
**Scope expanded 2026-05-01 (Aaron approved):** original card was "warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by". New scope: preserve original attribution per-review when the User can't be resolved on import.

**Why scope grew:** the agent framing ("warn and proceed with nil FK") still loses WHO triaged on cross-instance restore. Federal compliance audit posture requires the attribution chain to survive. Researched GitLab's post-migration mapping (creates placeholder User records + reassignment join table — overkill for Vulcan's one-shot backup/restore use case) and Discourse's external_id pattern (different problem). For Vulcan's scale: 4 columns on Review carry the original email + name when the User doesn't exist on the target.

**Schema** (4 nullable string columns on `reviews`):
- `triage_set_by_imported_email`
- `triage_set_by_imported_name`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_email`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_name`

**Behavior:**
- Resolve User → set FK as today, leave imported_* nil
- Can't resolve → leave FK nil, populate imported_* from archive JSON
- Display: fall back to imported_* with annotation when FK is nil
- Disposition export: same fallback in CSV cells
- Audit: imported_* columns NOT in vulcan_audited only: list (set once at import, never edited)

**References (consulted before deciding):**
- https://docs.gitlab.com/development/user_contribution_mapping/
- https://docs.gitlab.com/user/import/mapping/

**Touch points:**
- New migration: 4 nullable string columns on reviews
- `app/services/import/json_archive/review_builder.rb`: populate imported_* when resolve_user returns nil but archive has email/name
- `app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb`: expose imported_* fields + computed triager_label / adjudicator_label
- `app/lib/disposition_matrix_export.rb`: fallback in build_row for "Triaged By"/"Adjudicated By"
- `app/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.vue` + `ComponentComments.vue`: render fallback with "imported, no account" annotation
- Tests: importer (3 specs), display (2 specs), export (2 specs)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration adds 4 nullable string cols: triage_set_by_imported_email/name + adjudicated_by_imported_email/name
- [ ] When import resolves user successfully, imported_* cols stay nil
- [ ] When import can't resolve user but archive has email/name, imported_* cols populated and FK left nil
- [ ] When import can't resolve and archive has NO email/name, imported_* cols stay nil (no synthesized data)
- [ ] Display falls back to imported_* with "imported, no account on this instance" annotation
- [ ] Disposition CSV export falls back to imported_* in Triaged By + Adjudicated By cells
- [ ] No regression on existing import specs
- [ ] imported_* cols NOT in vulcan_audited only: list
- [ ] Warning still recorded in import result (carries the email + which review)
notes:
[2026-05-01 backend complete in commit b1ce575]
- Migration: 4 cols added to reviews
- Importer: populates imported_* when User can't resolve, adds warning
- Disposition export: falls back to imported_* in Triaged By + Adjudicated By cells with "(imported, no account: <email>)" annotation
- Tests: 87/87 green (importer 47 + disposition 40)

PENDING for next session:
- ReviewBlueprint: expose imported_* fields + computed triager_label / adjudicator_label
- Vue display: fallback rendering in CommentTriageModal + ComponentComments with "imported, no account" annotation
- Tests: blueprint spec + vitest for the 2 components
[2026-05-01 frontend complete in commit fafb71b]
- Review model: 4 new methods (triager_display_name, triager_imported?, adjudicator_display_name, adjudicator_imported?) — single source of truth for the FK→imported_* fallback.
- ReviewBlueprint: exposes the four + triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at for the modal.
- Component#paginated_comments + Project#paginated_comments: row hash includes the four display fields so the modal has the data on open (no extra fetch).
- CommentTriageModal.vue: renders "Triaged by ... · time" and "Adjudicated by ... · time" lines conditioned on triage_set_at / adjudicated_at, with a "imported" warning badge when *_imported is true.
- Tests: 14 new on Review model, 6 on ReviewBlueprint (refind: true on the let_it_be to avoid update_columns in-memory cache), 3 on paginated_comments, 6 on CommentTriageModal vitest.
- Visual verification with Playwright on /components/1/triage covering all 4 cases (imported triager only, resolved triager only, imported adjudicator + resolved triager mixed, and the placeholder/empty case). Screenshots in .beads/screenshots/pr717-8-*.png.

ALL acceptance criteria met. Closing.

title: Add project membership check to withdraw + update (security gap)
description: `app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb:10` excludes `withdraw` from `:set_project_from_review` only-list. Combined with line 16 `:authorize_review_owner` (which only checks `@review.user_id == current_user.id`), a user removed from the project (or whose project visibility was revoked) can still withdraw + update their own pending comments. Same for `update`. The comment block at line 17-19 claims `@project is set` by `set_project_from_review` — that's false for `withdraw`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `withdraw` added to `:set_project_from_review` only-list
- [ ] `update` review owner check confirmed paired with viewer-project gate
- [ ] Defensive `authorize_viewer_project` chained before owner-equality check
- [ ] Test: user removed from project gets 403 on withdraw of own pending comment
- [ ] Test: user removed from project gets 403 on update of own pending comment
- [ ] Comment block at L17-19 corrected
close_reason: Fixed in 08f56ac per policy 'off the project = no actions' (Aaron 2026-05-01). withdraw added to set_project_from_review; authorize_viewer_project added to withdraw+update. TDD 2 RED → 2 GREEN, 86/86 reviews_spec regression green.

title: PR-717 Public Comment Review — post-merge remediation
description:
# PR-717 Public Comment Review — Post-merge review remediation

This epic tracks all 45 findings from the 6-agent specialist review of PR-717 (branch `feat/viewer-comments`, tip `50a01a9`). The review covered Security, Test quality, Migration safety, DRY/architecture, API consistency, and Performance.

## Tier breakdown (45 cards total)

- **Ship blockers (P0, 5 cards: .1–.5)** — must fix before merge to master. Data-correctness regression on legacy reviews, production migration lock-up risk, CSV/Excel formula injection, double-cascade incoherence, broken toast variant.
- **High priority (P1, 8 cards: .6–.13)** — auth gap on withdraw, audit trail gaps, json_archive validator bypass + silent FK loss + audit-laundering chain, 3 TIER-1 test rewrites.
- **Medium (P2, 9 cards: .14–.22)** — DRY refactors (audit_comment filter + toast helper), perf index, response-shape canonicalization, ReviewBlueprint expansion, vocabulary drift detection, audit_comment length cap, stray-param validator.
- **Low (P3, 23 cards: .23–.45)** — broken out by domain:
  - Tests (.23–.30): 3 TIER-2 DOM-binding gaps + 5 TIER-3 missing edge cases
  - Performance (.31–.35): redundant joins, deep-thread audit blowup, nested-IN flatten, counter cache, per-component memoization
  - DRY (.36–.38): Picker.vue (deferred until N=3), AuditCommentForm extraction, 4-file FormMixin import cleanup
  - API (.39–.42): create returns review payload, JS const rename, admin_ prefix decision, row-shape unification across 3 endpoints
  - Security (.43–.44): Content-Disposition filename sanitization, rack_attack throttles on triage/admin endpoints
  - Migration (.45): move parked Task 31 migration out of db/

## Execution order (by priority + within-tier prerequisites)

1. **P0 blockers first** (gate to merge): all 5 parallel; .1 + .4 need design decisions before code work.
2. **P1 high** (post-merge sweep): .6, .7, .8, .9, .11, .12, .13 parallel; .10 depends on .7 + .9 (audit-laundering = associated_with + insert!-validation).
3. **P2 medium** (dedicated cleanup PR): .14, .15, .17–.22 parallel; .16 (length cap) depends on .14 (require_audit_comment filter).
4. **P3 low** (backlog): .39 (create returns review) depends on .20 (expand blueprint); rest parallel.

## Decision points (need Aaron input)

- `.1` — legacy `reviews.triage_status` default: NULL+nullable+scope-fix vs `'legacy'` sentinel vs scope-by-comment-period-start
- `.4` — which side owns the cascade: Rails `dependent: :destroy` (FK becomes `:restrict`) vs Postgres FK (Rails callback removed)
- `.41` — `admin_` prefix consistency: rename move_to_rule or document the rule

## Validation references

- `git log master..feat/viewer-comments --oneline` — 139 commits + delta
- 2124 backend specs / 2276 frontend specs / 0 failures pre-remediation
- See `docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/00-SESSION-2026-05-01-roadmap.md` for the original-PR scope
- 6 agent review reports: archived under `.beads/archive/` if needed

## Plan

User intent (Aaron, 2026-05-01): "fix all issues through medium ... then we can see how and if we want to do the lows"
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 5 ship-blocker cards (P0) closed
- [ ] All 8 high-priority cards (P1) closed
- [ ] All 9 medium cards (P2) closed
- [ ] PR-717 merged cleanly to master
- [ ] No regression on the 2124+2276 test sweep
- [ ] Container SRG public comment workflow operating in prod

title: Inherited-requirements as first-class workflow (PR #717 Task 32)
description:
DISA Vendor STIG Process v4r1 §4.1.15 prescribes the inheritance pattern as: status=Applicable-Does Not Meet + Mitigation field with canonical sentence "This requirement is fully mitigated by [parent-STIG-RuleID]" + Status Justification. Vulcan today writes to the wrong field (vendor_comments), picks the wrong status (auto-overrides to Configurable), and uses internal SV-ids instead of human-readable STIG-IDs. Authors have no UI button for "Mark as Inherited"; commenters get no badge.

Schema:
- base_rules.inherited_external_citation (string)
- base_rules.inheritance_justification (text)

Model:
- Rule#inherited? (satisfied_by.any? || external citation present)
- Validator: inherited → status must be DNM
- Validator: inherited → justification required
- Drop status auto-override at rule.rb:140
- New Rule#mitigation_export_text helper

Export:
- Spreadsheet: Mitigation column carries citation; Status Justification carries justification
- XCCDF: verify DISA placement (likely vendor-specific extension)
- Disposition CSV (Task 29): add Status + Mitigation columns

UI:
- "Mark as Inherited" button + modal (two tabs: from-Vulcan-rule cross-project picker / from-external-STIG)
- Justification textarea required, min 50 chars
- Inherited badge on rule list/editor
- Commenter view: badge + hint "consider commenting on the parent canonical"

PR-717 integration:
- Mark-as-duplicate picker (Task 24): suggest parent canonical when current rule is inherited
- Disposition CSV: add Status + Mitigation columns

Acceptance:

title: H4: Batch access_request lookup in ProjectsController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/projects_controller.rb:24-33`

**Problem:** Per-project `current_user.access_requests.find_by(project_id:)` is N+1. 50 projects = 50 queries.

**Fix:** Batch: `ar_by_project = current_user.access_requests.where(project_id: project_ids).index_by(&:project_id)` then hash lookup.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone controller fix.

title: H5: Add eager_load to ComponentsController#find rule serialization
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/components_controller.rb:426`

**Problem:** `render json: rules` triggers full `Rule#as_json` without eager loading associations (reviews, satisfies, satisfied_by, srg_rule). After C3 is fixed, the SRG N+1 is gone, but the association N+1 remains.

**Fix:** Add `.eager_load(:reviews, :satisfies, :satisfied_by, :srg_rule)` to the rules query, or use `as_json(skip_merge: true)` for search results that don't need full detail.

**Depends on:** C3 (Rule#as_json fix).

- [ ] Test: find with 10 matching rules generates < 10 queries (not 50+)

title: H2: Add .limit() to unbounded user audit query in UsersController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:15-18`

**Problem:** `Audited.audit_class.where(auditable_type: 'User').map(&:format)` loads ALL user audit records without `.limit()`. Grows unbounded over time. On a mature instance, thousands of records materialized into Ruby.

**Fix:** Add `.limit(200)` before `.map(&:format)`.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Audit query has .limit(200) or similar
- [ ] Test: with 500 audit records, only 200 returned

title: H3: Consolidate Project#details from 9 COUNT queries to 1-2
description:
**Location:** `app/models/project.rb:62-73`

**Problem:** 9 separate `rules.where(status: ...).size` queries. Each goes through `has_many :rules, through: :components` join. Called on every project show page.

**Fix:** Single query: `counts = rules.group(:status).count` for status counts, plus `rules.where(locked: false).where(review_requestor_id: nil).count` etc. Reduces 9 queries to 2-3.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone model fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Project#details uses GROUP BY instead of 9 separate queries
- [ ] Test: details returns same values as before (regression)
- [ ] Test: only 1-3 SQL queries generated (not 9)
- [ ] Project show page specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
notes: [2026-04-07] Still needs GROUP BY consolidation. Not addressed by Blueprinter migration.
close_reason: Fixed: Project#details rewritten with group(:status).count + group(:locked).count + CASE WHEN for review counts. 9 queries → 3. Test verifies ≤4 queries.

title: H1: Optimize check_access_request_notifications (runs every request)
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:268-277`

**Problem:** `before_action` runs on EVERY request. Iterates all available projects, calls `can_admin_project?` per project (membership query each), loads access_requests with eager_load per admin project. For admin with 50 projects = 100+ queries before ANY page renders. Also runs on JSON API calls that don't even render the navbar.

**Fix:**
1. Skip for JSON format: `return @access_requests if request.format.json?`
2. Single query: `ProjectAccessRequest.joins(:project => :memberships).where(memberships: { user_id: current_user.id, role: 'admin' }).eager_load(:user, :project)`
3. Or cache result in session with short TTL

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix in ApplicationController.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Does NOT run N+1 per project
- [ ] Skips for JSON format requests
- [ ] Uses single query instead of iterating projects
- [ ] Test: admin with 10 projects generates < 5 queries (not 20+)
- [ ] Test: JSON API requests don't trigger notification check
- [ ] All request specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
close_reason: Closed

title: Restore prev_unconfirmed_email capture for reconfirmation flash message
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:29`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```

**Problem:** This is a no-op — reads `unconfirmed_email` and throws away the value. Compare to stock Devise which does:
```ruby
prev_unconfirmed_email = resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```
and then passes `prev_unconfirmed_email` to `set_flash_message_for_update(resource, prev_unconfirmed_email)` to tell the user "We sent a confirmation link to your new email address" vs "Profile updated successfully."

**Fix:** Either capture to a local and use it for flash messaging, or delete the line entirely if the simplified flash is intentional.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: user changes email → flash says 'confirmation link sent to new address' (not generic 'profile updated')
- [ ] Request spec: user does not change email → flash says 'profile updated'
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] Cross-check with Devise stock RegistrationsController#update to match behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Slack notification firing on every user update instead of only admin changes
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:70-72`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
if @user.update(user_update_params)
  notification_type = @user.admin ? :assign_vulcan_admin : :remove_vulcan_admin
  send_slack_notification(notification_type, @user) if Settings.slack.enabled
```

**Problem:** Every successful user update fires a Slack notification as either "assign_vulcan_admin" or "remove_vulcan_admin" — regardless of whether the admin flag actually changed. A simple name change broadcasts "User demoted from vulcan admin" to Slack.

**Fix:** Gate on `@user.saved_change_to_admin?`.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Slack spam damages trust in notifications; false "promoted/demoted" messages confuse ops team.
**How to apply:** Only notify when admin flag actually changed, not on every update. TDD required.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: update name only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: update email only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to true → :assign_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to false → :remove_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: update name + toggle admin → exactly one Slack call (admin change)
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] No regressions in users_controller_spec
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix polymorphic audit query missing user_type filter in registrations#edit
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:11-12`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
@histories = Audited.audit_class.includes(:user)
                    .where(user_id: current_user.id)
```

**Problem:** `Audited::Audit#user` is a polymorphic association (`user_id` + `user_type` columns). Filtering on `user_id` alone is incorrect for polymorphic data. Today every actor is a User so IDs don't collide, but `VulcanAudit.create_initial_rule_audit_from_mapping` already uses `user_type: 'System'`, which this query would leak if System shared an ID with current_user.

**Fix:** Add `user_type: 'User'` to the where clause.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Latent bug — breaks the moment any non-User actor shares an ID space with a User.
**How to apply:** TDD: create an audit with user_type='System' and same id; verify it's excluded from results.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with same user_id but user_type='System' → NOT returned in @histories
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with user_type='User' → returned in @histories
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] users_controller#index has same pattern — verify already correct (filters by auditable_type)
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch XLSX export from FastExcel to caxlsx for xml:space=preserve whitespace fidelity
description:
## Problem
FastExcel writes cells via `write_string()` — plain text only, no `xml:space="preserve"` attribute on `<t>` elements in the shared strings table. When Excel re-saves, whitespace in multiline markdown content (code blocks, indentation, blank lines) gets normalized, causing false-positive diffs on re-import (user edits 1 rule, system detects 9 changes).

## Root Cause
Research confirmed: the issue is NOT Excel modifying content, but the XLSX writer failing to mark whitespace for preservation. `xml:space="preserve"` on `<t>` elements tells XML parsers to keep all whitespace as-is.

## Solution
Switch from FastExcel to **caxlsx** (community axlsx) which:
- Sets `xml:space="preserve"` by default on shared strings
- Supports `use_shared_strings = true` for multiline content
- Supports `wrap_text: true` cell styling
- Supports data validation dropdowns (future: vulcan-clean-di3)

## Key Files
- `app/services/export/formatters/excel_formatter.rb` — current FastExcel writer
- `Gemfile` — swap gem dependency

## Research Sources
- caxlsx defaults `xml_space = :preserve` in SharedStringsTable
- FastExcel has no rich text or whitespace preservation support
- Excel, LibreOffice, Google Sheets all honor `xml:space="preserve"`
- Roo `_x000D_` conversion may double newlines from Windows Excel (test)
- No STIG ecosystem tool has solved this — Vulcan would be first
notes: [2026-02-22 11:30] Completed: Switched FastExcel → caxlsx gem. Added Source column (Direct/Inherited) to Excel exports with grey fill, locked cells, dropdowns (Status/Severity/Source), sheet protection, auto-filter. Fixed non-deterministic satisfied_by.first → satisfied_by.order(:id).first in export_checktext/export_fixtext. 343 tests pass, 0 failures. Live tested: real edit detected correctly. 8 inherited-row false positives still appear due to compute_rule_changes using csv_attributes which hits the same non-deterministic path at runtime. Next: consider skipping check/fix comparison for inherited rows in compute_rule_changes, OR live test again after .order(:id) fix applied to rule.rb.
close_reason: caxlsx swap complete with Source column, styling, dropdowns, sheet protection. 343 tests pass.

title: devise-security fork: session_traceable module
description:
## Context
AC-10 (V-222387) requires configurable per-user concurrent session limits. devise-security's :session_limitable hardcodes to 1. PR #442 by itsmechlark adds :session_traceable with max_active_sessions config + session_histories table. Stale since Feb 2024, 43 review comments.

## What Was Done (Phase 1 COMPLETE)

  - Extracted side effects from allow_limitable_authentication? → evict_oldest_session!
  - Removed session_traceable_adapter indirection (use AR associations directly)
  - Replaced Timecop → ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers
  - Removed mocha dependency (minitest stub only)
  - Added presence guard to update_traceable_token!

  - sqlite3 ~> 1.4 (Rails 7.0 compat, was ~> 2.8)
  - Devise 5 lowercased authentication_keys in i18n messages (3 test files)

  - Double constantize in log_traceable_session!
- **191 tests pass, 0 failures, 0 errors**

## Remaining (Phase 2: Vulcan Integration)

title: Quick security wins for v2.3.1 (rack-attack, XXE, limits)
description:
Quick security wins that can ship with v2.3.1.

## Work Items

1. **rack-attack** — gem install + initializer. Throttle login (5/min/IP), uploads (10/min/user). ~2 hrs.

2. **XXE one-line fix** — Remove NOENT from disa_rule_description.rb:29. Change to RECOVER | NONET. ~10 min.

3. **File size limits** — before_action on upload controllers. Check params[:file].size < MAX. ~1 hr.

4. **Input length limits** — Add validates :field, length: { maximum: N } to key models. ~1 hr.

## Why v2.3.1
These are low-risk, high-value hardening changes. No schema changes, no new gems beyond rack-attack, no behavioral changes for users. All are additive protections.

title: Schema validation for XCCDF (XSD), JSON Archive, CSV imports
description:
Add schema validation for structured file imports.

## Work Items

1. **XCCDF: Validate against official XSD** — XCCDF 1.2 has a NIST XSD schema. Use `Nokogiri::XML::Schema` to validate uploads before processing. Download/vendor the XSD from NIST SCAP repo.

2. **JSON Archive: Strict manifest schema** — Validate manifest.json structure before import:
   - backup_format_version in supported versions
   - components array with max item count (e.g., 100)
   - Each component: name (string, max 255), prefix (pattern), srg_id (string)
   - Rule data: validate locked_fields keys against LOCKABLE_SECTION_NAMES
   - Review action validated against allowed enum

3. **CSV formula injection sanitization** — Strip leading `=`, `+`, `-`, `@` from cell values on import. These execute as formulas when exported CSVs are opened in Excel by DISA reviewers.

4. **Zip bomb protection** — Check uncompressed entry sizes before reading from ZIP (JSON Archive + XLSX). Set max total decompressed size (e.g., 500 MB).

## Files
- app/lib/xccdf/ (XSD validation)
- app/services/import/json_archive/ (manifest + rule schema)
- app/models/component.rb (CSV sanitization in from_spreadsheet)
- db/seeds/schemas/ (vendor the XCCDF XSD)

## Tests
- Spec: invalid XCCDF fails XSD validation with clear error
- Spec: malformed manifest rejected with specific field errors
- Spec: formula-prefixed cell values are sanitized
- Spec: zip bomb detected and rejected
notes: DISA STIGs use XCCDF 1.1 (not 1.2). Schema: xccdf-1.1.4.xsd. Already available at ../cis-bench/schemas/xccdf-1.1.4.xsd plus dependencies (cpe, simpledc, xml.xsd, platform). Vendor these into db/seeds/schemas/ or lib/schemas/. Both 1.1 and 1.2 XSDs available if needed.

title: EPIC: Input Security Hardening (v2.3.2+)
description: Harden all input boundaries: XCCDF XML, JSON Archive, CSV/XLSX uploads. Three phases: critical fixes, schema validation, infrastructure hardening. See child cards for details.

title: Per-section lock UX: field state visualization
description:
## Problem
Section-locked fields look identical to whole-rule-locked and under-review fields — all just grey/disabled. Users can't tell WHY a field is disabled.

## Solution
Add distinct colored left-border indicators to form groups:
- **Yellow** (`#ffc107`) — section locked by reviewer
- **Blue** (`#17a2b8`) — under review, awaiting approval  
- **Grey** (`#6c757d`) — whole-rule locked via review system
- **Default** — editable

Add a small legend component above the form when any non-default state is active.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section-locked fields have yellow left border + lock icon
- [ ] Under-review fields have blue left border
- [ ] Whole-rule-locked fields have grey left border (existing disabled look)
- [ ] Legend shows active states with color key
- [ ] Visual states apply to RuleForm, DisaRuleDescriptionForm, CheckForm
- [ ] Mount tests verify correct CSS classes per state
- [ ] All 1807+ frontend tests pass
close_reason: Closed

title: CSV/XLSX round-trip: export, edit, re-import to update component
description:
Ensure CSV/XLSX round-trip works for the primary authoring workflow:

1. User exports component as CSV or Excel (working_copy mode)
2. User edits rules in Excel/Google Sheets (bulk edits)
3. User re-imports the spreadsheet to UPDATE the existing component

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export CSV/XLSX → edit → re-import updates existing rules (not just creates new component)
- Field mapping alignment: export headers match import expectations (or aliases handle it)
- Satisfaction relationships preserved through round-trip
- InSpec control body preserved if present
- No data loss on round-trip for editable fields

## Out of Scope
- JSON Archive round-trip (already complete, machine-to-machine only)
- XCCDF round-trip (read-only reference format)
- InSpec import (separate card: vulcan-clean-9du)

## Current State (2026-02-19)

title: Bug: DISA Excel multi-component export produces blank worksheets
description: When exporting multiple components via DISA Excel (VendorSubmission + Excel), the worksheets in the resulting .xlsx are blank despite correct worksheet names. Single-component exports work fine. The issue is likely in ExcelFormatter.generate_workbook or how FastExcel handles constant_memory mode with multiple sheets. Debug by comparing single vs multi-component paths in Export::Base.export_as_workbook. The old ExportHelper.export_excel works correctly for multi-component — diff the two approaches.
close_reason: Fixed: ExcelFormatter called read_string before close in constant_memory mode. Swapping order fixed all blank worksheet exports.

title: CSV header alignment: export headers != import headers
description:
STIG/SRG CSV export uses `BENCHMARK_CSV_COLUMNS` headers:
- `Title`, `Description`, `Check`, `Fix`, `STIG ID`, `SRG ID`, etc.

Component spreadsheet import expects `IMPORT_MAPPING` headers:
- `Requirement`, `VulDiscussion`, `Check`, `Fix`, `STIGID`, `SRGID`, etc.

Header names don't align, so you can't export a STIG CSV and import it as a Component without renaming columns manually.

## Approach Options
1. **Make import accept both header sets** — import recognizes aliases (e.g., `Title` OR `Requirement`)
2. **Add Component CSV export with import-compatible headers** — separate "round-trip" format
3. **Align all headers to one standard** — breaking change for existing users

Option 1 is safest (Postel's Law again — liberal import).

## Tests
- Round-trip test: export Component CSV → re-import as new Component → verify field fidelity
- Import with BENCHMARK_CSV_COLUMNS headers → should succeed
- Import with IMPORT_MAPPING headers → should still succeed (backwards compat)
close_reason: Already fixed in commit f54d67a. HEADER_ALIASES in import_constants.rb + normalize_import_headers in component.rb. Tests at components_spec.rb:125 (aliases) and :525 (round-trip).

title: Search quality testing: ambiguity, common patterns, fuzzing
description:
## Problem
Global search returns results from 7 categories (Projects, Components, Rules, SRGs, STIGs, STIG Rules, SRG Rules). Users may click wrong category when same term appears in multiple places.

Example: Search "CIS" might match:
- Component: "CIS Benchmark for RHEL 9" (user's project)
- STIG: "CIS Controls Reference" (published doc)

User clicks wrong one → confusion.

## Test Gaps Identified

### 1. Ambiguity Tests (same name, multiple categories)
- Search "RHEL" with RHEL Component AND RHEL STIG → verify both appear in correct sections
- Search "Windows" with Windows Component AND Windows STIG → verify routing works
- Verify UI clearly distinguishes categories

### 2. Common Security Pattern Tests
- `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` → should find STIG rules with check content
- `CCI-000366` → should find rules with that CCI
- `password complexity` → should find relevant rules
- `audit log` → common security term
- `firewall` → matches many rules

### 3. Fuzzing/Edge Cases
- Very long queries (500+ chars)
- Special characters: `&`, `<`, `>`, quotes, backticks
- SQL injection attempts: `'; DROP TABLE--`
- XSS attempts: `<script>alert(1)</script>`
- Unicode characters
- Empty/whitespace queries

title: Unify controls page layout with Composition API
description:
## Objective
Unify the controls display between `/components/:id` (overview) and `/components/:id/controls` (edit) using Vue 2.7 Composition API and composables, following TDD.

## Architecture

### New Components (Composition API)
1. **ControlsPageLayout.vue** - Shared three-column layout with slots
2. **RuleCommandBar.vue** - Action buttons, extracted from RulesCodeEditorView

### New Composables
1. **useRuleFilters.js** - Filter state & toggle logic
2. **useRuleSelection.js** - Selected rule management (replaces SelectedRulesMixin)
3. **useSidebar.js** - Sidebar open/close state
4. **useRuleActions.js** - Save, lock, review, clone, delete actions

### Existing Components (Keep Options API)
- RuleNavigator.vue - Left sidebar
- RuleFilterBar.vue - Filter switches
- RuleForm.vue - Documentation form
- RuleEditor.vue - Tab container
- Sidebar components (RuleSatisfactions, RuleReviews, RuleHistories)

## Implementation Order (TDD)

### Phase 1: Composables
1. Write tests for useRuleSelection composable
2. Implement useRuleSelection
3. Write tests for useRuleFilters composable
4. Implement useRuleFilters
5. Write tests for useSidebar composable
6. Implement useSidebar
7. Write tests for useRuleActions composable
8. Implement useRuleActions

### Phase 2: Layout Component
1. Write tests for ControlsPageLayout
2. Implement ControlsPageLayout with slots
3. Write tests for RuleCommandBar
4. Implement RuleCommandBar

### Phase 3: Integration
1. Refactor RulesCodeEditorView to use new layout + composables
2. Refactor RulesReadOnlyView to use same layout with readOnly=true
3. Integration tests for both views

### Phase 4: Cleanup
1. Remove SelectedRulesMixin (replaced by composable)
2. Remove duplicate code
3. Fix command bar responsive layout issue

## File Structure

```
app/javascript/
├── components/
│   └── rules/
│       ├── ControlsPageLayout.vue (new)
│       ├── RuleCommandBar.vue (new)
│       ├── RulesCodeEditorView.vue (refactor)
│       └── RulesReadOnlyView.vue (refactor)
├── composables/
│   ├── useRuleFilters.js (new)
│   ├── useRuleSelection.js (new)
│   ├── useSidebar.js (new)
│   └── useRuleActions.js (new)
└── __tests__/
    ├── composables/
    │   ├── useRuleFilters.spec.js
    │   ├── useRuleSelection.spec.js
    │   ├── useSidebar.spec.js
    │   └── useRuleActions.spec.js
    └── components/
        ├── ControlsPageLayout.spec.js
        └── RuleCommandBar.spec.js
```

## Props for ControlsPageLayout

```javascript
props: {
  readOnly: Boolean,        // Disable editing
  showCommandBar: Boolean,  // Show/hide command bar
  showFilterBar: Boolean,   // Show/hide filter bar
}
```

## Slots for ControlsPageLayout

- `header` - Breadcrumb and title area
- `command-bar` - Action buttons (optional)
- `filter-bar` - Filter switches (optional)
- `left-sidebar` - RuleNavigator
- `main-content` - RuleEditor/RuleForm
- `right-panels` - Sidebar slideovers

## Testing Strategy

- **Composables**: Pure function tests with @vue/test-utils
- **Components**: Mount tests with slot injection
- **Integration**: Full page render with mocked API

## Success Criteria

1. Both views share same layout component
2. All composables have 100% test coverage
3. Command bar responsive issue fixed
4. No regression in existing functionality
5. Code ready for Vue 3 migration

title: Fix Remember Me checkbox on login page
description:
## Bug Report
"Remember Me" checkbox on login page does not work correctly.

## Investigation Findings

### What's configured:
- User model includes `:rememberable` devise module ✓
- Login form has `f.check_box :remember_me` ✓
- Default `remember_for = 2.weeks` (not explicitly set, using default) ✓
- `expire_all_remember_me_on_sign_out = true` is set

### Possible causes to investigate:
1. **Secure cookies issue** - If app served over HTTP but cookies marked secure
2. **Timeoutable module** - User model has `:timeoutable` which may override remember me
3. **Session expiration** - Check if session timeout is shorter than remember period
4. **Cookie domain/path** - May not be set correctly for the environment
5. **Browser-specific** - Some browsers block third-party cookies

### Files to check:
- `config/initializers/devise.rb` - line 137 (remember_for), line 140 (expire on sign out)
- `app/models/user.rb` - line 6 (timeoutable), line 12 (rememberable)
- `app/views/devise/sessions/_local.html.haml` - checkbox implementation

### Likely fix:
Check if `:timeoutable` timeout is shorter than remember period, or if running on HTTP with secure cookie settings.

title: Docker env defaults and admin bootstrap
description:
## Problem

Docker deployments require `.env` file to exist, breaking "works out of box" experience.
`docker compose up` and `vulcan build --info` fail without `.env`.

## Decisions Made

### 1. Dockerfile Core Defaults
Bake sensible defaults into image so it starts without any config:

```dockerfile
ENV RAILS_ENV="production" \
    RAILS_SERVE_STATIC_FILES="true" \
    RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT="true" \
    RAILS_FORCE_SSL="false" \
    PORT=3000 \
    POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
    POSTGRES_DB=vulcan_postgres_production \
    DATABASE_PORT=5432 \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_LOCAL_LOGIN="true" \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_OIDC="false" \
    VULCAN_ENABLE_LDAP="false" \
    VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN="true"
```

### 2. docker-compose.yml
Make `.env` optional (override mechanism, not requirement):

```yaml
env_file:
  - path: .env
    required: false
```

### 3. Admin Bootstrap (Priority Order)

1. **Explicit admin via env vars** (highest priority):
   - `VULCAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` + `VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD`
   - Created on db:prepare if no admin exists

2. **First user becomes admin** (default behavior):
   - `VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=true` (default)
   - First login (local/OIDC/OAuth/LDAP) gets admin
   - Makes app functional immediately

3. **Manual admin** (opt-out):
   - Set `VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=false`
   - Use rails console to create admin

### 4. Deployment Targets
- Docker Compose: uses .env for overrides
- Helm/K8s: ConfigMaps/Secrets override Dockerfile defaults
- Heroku: `heroku config:set` overrides defaults

## Still To Decide
- Password generation for explicit admin (if VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD not set)
- Password reset mechanisms
- Applies to both v2.2.x and v2.3.0

## Files to Modify
- Dockerfile (both repos)
- docker-compose.yml (both repos)
- db/seeds.rb or new rake task
- app/models/user.rb (omniauth callback)
- ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md
close_reason: Done — commits dcb296d (config defaults aligned), e3e6b20 (New Project button fix), admin bootstrap already existed. Settings docs in docs/getting-started/configuration.md

comments.author: Aaron Lippold
comments.text:
## Database Password Standardization (Added)

### Problem
Two different env vars for database password:
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` - used by docker-compose for postgres container
- `VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD` - used by Rails database.yml (legacy)

This caused confusion and potential misconfiguration.

### Decision
Standardize on 12-factor pattern:
1. `DATABASE_URL` takes precedence (Heroku, K8s, docker-compose)
2. Fallback to individual vars: `POSTGRES_USER`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `POSTGRES_DB`
3. `VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD` deprecated but still supported for backward compatibility

### Files Changed
- `config/database.yml` - Added DATABASE_URL support with fallback chain
- `ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md` - Updated database section, deprecation note
- `docs/getting-started/environment-variables.md` - Same

### New database.yml production section
```yaml
production:
  <<: *default
  url: <%= ENV['DATABASE_URL'] %>
  host: <%= ENV.fetch('DATABASE_HOST', 'localhost') %>
  port: <%= ENV.fetch('DATABASE_PORT', 5432) %>
  database: <%= ENV.fetch('POSTGRES_DB', 'vulcan_postgres_production') %>
  username: <%= ENV.fetch('POSTGRES_USER', 'postgres') %>
  password: <%= ENV['POSTGRES_PASSWORD'] || ENV['VULCAN_VUE_DATABASE_PASSWORD'] %>
```

comments.author: Aaron Lippold
comments.text:
## Revised Implementation Plan (Based on Research)

### Research Findings
- No major Rails app (Discourse, GitLab, Mastodon) uses auto-first-user-admin
- WordPress installer race condition attacks are documented and real
- All major apps use explicit CLI/rake tasks for admin bootstrap
- Race conditions need DB-level protection (advisory locks)

### Hybrid Approach (Priority Order)

1. **VULCAN_ADMIN_EMAIL + VULCAN_ADMIN_PASSWORD** (Primary - Most Secure)
   - Runs on db:prepare/startup
   - Explicit, auditable, no race condition
   - Works for Docker, K8s, Heroku

2. **VULCAN_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=true** (Fallback - Convenience)
   - First user becomes admin WITH advisory lock
   - Prevents race condition vulnerability
   - Good for demos/quick evaluations

3. **rake db:create_admin** (Manual - Existing)
   - Keep for backward compatibility

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1: Env Var Bootstrap**
- New rake task or initializer
- Creates admin from env vars on startup
- Idempotent (skips if admin exists)
- TDD: Write tests first

**Phase 2: First-User-Admin with Advisory Lock**
- Wrap after_create in advisory lock
- Prevents race condition
- TDD: Write tests first

**Phase 3: Integration Testing**
- Test priority order
- Test concurrent registration (race condition prevented)
- Full test suite passes

**Phase 4: Documentation**
- Update ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md
- Update docs/getting-started/
- Update docker-compose examples
- Update .env.example files

title: Unify password complexity requirements across frontend/backend
description:
## Problem
Password complexity requirements are currently:
- Hardcoded in frontend (PasswordInput.vue)
- Potentially different in backend (Devise validation)
- Not configurable per deployment
- User reported they're "not in sync throughout the app"

## Current Frontend Requirements (PasswordInput.vue)
```
• At least 8 characters (12+ recommended)
• Uppercase and lowercase letters
• Numbers and special characters
```

## What Needs to Happen

1. **Backend Configuration** (Single Source of Truth)
   - Define requirements in Settings/ENV (min_length, require_uppercase, etc.)
   - Implement Devise custom validator using these settings
   - Expose via API endpoint: GET /api/auth/password_requirements

2. **Frontend Reads from Backend**
   - PasswordInput.vue fetches requirements from API
   - Dynamically displays rules based on backend config
   - Strength calculator uses backend rules

3. **Configurable Per Deployment**
   - Add to config/vulcan.default.yml
   - ENV vars: VULCAN_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH, etc.
   - Different deployments can set different policies

## Files to Check/Modify
- `app/javascript/components/auth/PasswordInput.vue` (hardcoded rules)
- `app/models/user.rb` (Devise validations)
- `config/vulcan.default.yml` (add password policy settings)
- `app/controllers/api/auth/password_requirements_controller.rb` (new - expose config)

## References
- User feedback: "password complexity system not in sync"
- Current implementation: lines 35-66 in PasswordInput.vue
notes:
[2026-02-19] Research complete. Current state:
- Backend: Devise `:validatable` = 6-128 chars only, no complexity
- Frontend: PasswordField.vue = display wrapper only, zero validation
- No password settings in vulcan.default.yml
- HAML views show "(6 characters minimum)" hint only

Plan for next session:
1. Add `vuelidate@0.7.7` (Vue 2 compatible, BootstrapVue recommended)
2. Add password policy to vulcan.default.yml (min_length, require_uppercase, require_lowercase, require_number, require_special)
3. Create PasswordPolicyValidator (custom Devise validator reading Settings)
4. Expose policy via Settings.password (already available in HAML layout)
5. Enhance PasswordField.vue with real-time validation using Vuelidate
6. TDD: write specs first (model validation, request spec, Vitest component)
7. Use let_it_be for expensive setup, @test/testHelper for DRY test infra

Key files:
- app/models/user.rb (add custom validator)
- config/initializers/devise.rb (password_length from Settings)
- config/vulcan.default.yml (password policy section)
- app/javascript/components/shared/PasswordField.vue (add Vuelidate)
- app/views/devise/registrations/_form.html.haml (pass policy to Vue)
- app/views/devise/passwords/edit.html.haml (pass policy to Vue)
close_reason: Password policy unification complete: count-based DoD 2222 defaults, backend validator, frontend checklist, docs

title: Migrate Login/Auth to SPA with TDD
description:
# Migrate Login/Auth to SPA with TDD

## Problem
- Current server-rendered login has test failures (redirect loops in test environment)
- Login2.vue exists but doesn't fit our design system
- Mixed server/client authentication creates complexity

## Solution
Migrate authentication to SPA using established pattern (API → Store → Composable → Page).

## Design Direction (Based on Research)

### Layout Pattern (Inspired by NuxtUI AuthForm)
**Vertical Stack Layout:**
1. **Header**: Logo + "Sign in to Vulcan" title
2. **Provider Buttons**: OIDC, GitHub, LDAP (full-width, stacked)
3. **Divider**: "or continue with email" separator
4. **Form**: Email/password with floating labels (Bootstrap 5 pattern)
5. **Submit**: Full-width "Sign In" button
6. **Footer**: Links (forgot password, etc.)

### Bootstrap 5 Styling
- **Floating labels** for modern form UX
- **btn-outline-secondary** for provider buttons with brand icons
- **btn-primary** for submit button
- **alert-danger** for validation errors
- **Responsive** card layout (max-w-md equivalent)
- **Clean, minimal** design matching existing SPA pages

### Component Structure
```
LoginPage.vue (page wrapper)
├─ AuthLayout.vue (centered card layout)
   ├─ LoginHeader.vue (logo + title)
   ├─ AuthProviderButtons.vue (OIDC/GitHub/LDAP)
   ├─ Divider.vue ("or" separator)
   ├─ LoginForm.vue (email/password with floating labels)
   └─ LoginFooter.vue (links)
```

### Key Features
- Loading states on buttons during authentication
- Real-time validation feedback
- Keyboard navigation support (Enter to submit)
- Accessible (proper labels, ARIA attributes)
- Mobile-optimized touch targets
- Matches Bootstrap 5 design system used in SPA

## Architecture (TDD)

### 1. Backend API (Already Exists)
SessionsController#create already has JSON support:
```ruby
format.json do
  self.resource = warden.authenticate!(auth_options)
  sign_in(resource_name, resource)
  render json: { user: resource.as_json(only: %i[id email admin]) }, status: :ok
end
```

**Add Tests:**
- Request specs for JSON login flow
- Error cases: invalid credentials, locked account, etc.
- Multiple providers: local, OIDC, GitHub, LDAP

### 2. API Layer
`app/javascript/apis/sessions.ts`
```typescript
export const sessionsApi = {
  login: (email: string, password: string) => 
    axios.post('/users/sign_in.json', { user: { email, password } }),
  logout: () => 
    axios.delete('/users/sign_out.json'),
  currentUser: () => 
    axios.get('/api/current_user.json')
}
```

**Tests:** Mock HTTP responses, error handling

### 3. Pinia Store
`app/javascript/stores/auth.store.ts`
- State: currentUser, loading, error
- Actions: login, logout, checkAuth
- Getters: isAuthenticated, isAdmin

**Tests:** Store actions, mutations, getters

### 4. Composable
`app/javascript/composables/useAuth.ts`
- Wraps auth store
- Business logic for login/logout
- Error formatting
- Redirect handling

**Tests:** Login flow, error states, redirects

### 5. Page & Components
`app/javascript/pages/auth/LoginPage.vue`
- Bootstrap 5 design (NOT Login2.vue style)
- Supports all auth providers
- Loading states, error messages
- Responsive design

**Components:**
- `LoginForm.vue` - Email/password form
- `AuthProviderButtons.vue` - OIDC/GitHub/LDAP buttons
- `AuthLayout.vue` - Shared layout for auth pages

**Tests:** Component tests for each

### 6. Router Integration
Update Vue Router with `/login` route
Guard for authenticated routes
Redirect after login

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass (backend + frontend)
- [ ] Login works with all providers (local, OIDC, GitHub, LDAP)
- [ ] Design fits Bootstrap 5 system (floating labels, clean minimal)
- [ ] No more server-rendered login quirks
- [ ] Full TDD coverage
- [ ] Solves sessions_spec.rb test failure

## Dependencies
- Existing SessionsController JSON endpoints
- Bootstrap 5 UI components
- Pinia store infrastructure
- Vue Router setup

## Estimate
8-12 hours (TDD, design implementation, all providers, testing)

## References
- NuxtUI AuthForm pattern: https://ui.nuxt.com/components/auth-form
- Bootstrap 5 floating labels: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/floating-labels/
- Rodauth migration planned after stabilization

## Notes
- Login2.vue exists but doesn't fit design system - use as reference only
- Follow API → Store → Composable → Page architecture
- Backend-agnostic design supports future Devise → Rodauth migration
- This permanently fixes the redirect loop test failures

comments.author: alippold
comments.text:
# Phase 1: Backend API Test Plan

## Current State Analysis

### SessionsController#create (POST /users/sign_in)
✅ JSON support exists (lines 22-26)
- Returns: `{ user: { id, email, admin } }`
- Missing tests for JSON format

### SessionsController#destroy (DELETE /users/sign_out)  
❌ NO JSON support - only HTML redirects
- Needs: JSON format handler
- Returns: 204 No Content (standard for logout)

### Current User Endpoint
❌ Does NOT exist
- Needs: GET /api/current_user
- Should inherit from Api::BaseController
- Returns: `{ user: { id, email, admin } }` or 401

## Tests to Write (TDD - RED phase)

### 1. JSON Login Tests (sessions_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'POST /users/sign_in.json'
  - Success: valid credentials → 200 + user JSON
  - Error: invalid credentials → 401
  - Error: missing email → 400
  - Error: missing password → 400
```

### 2. JSON Logout Tests (sessions_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'DELETE /users/sign_out.json'
  - Success: authenticated user → 204 No Content
  - Success: clears session (verify with subsequent request)
  - No error for unauthenticated (logout is idempotent)
```

### 3. Current User Tests (spec/requests/api/current_user_spec.rb)
```ruby
describe 'GET /api/current_user'
  - Success: authenticated → 200 + user JSON
  - Error: unauthenticated → 401
```

## Implementation Plan (GREEN phase)

### Step 1: Add JSON to SessionsController#destroy
```ruby
def destroy
  respond_to do |format|
    format.json do
      sign_out(resource_name)
      head :no_content
    end
    format.html do
      # existing OIDC logic...
    end
  end
end
```

### Step 2: Create Api::CurrentUserController
```ruby
class Api::CurrentUserController < Api::BaseController
  def show
    if current_user
      render json: { user: current_user.as_json(only: %i[id email admin]) }
    else
      head :unauthorized
    end
  end
end
```

### Step 3: Add route
```ruby
# config/routes.rb
namespace :api do
  resource :current_user, only: [:show]
end
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All new tests written (RED)
- [ ] Tests fail as expected
- [ ] Implementation added (GREEN)
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No existing tests broken
