title: Add test: POST comment review during adjudication phase is rejected
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb:734-764` phase enforcement loop (line 710) tests `draft / adjudication / final` for posting. For `adjudication`, only `triage` action is tested (line 750). Per the file's design comment at line 678 ("no NEW public comments" in adjudication), need a test that POST `/rules/:rule_id/reviews` with `action: 'comment'` is REJECTED in adjudication phase.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Component in adjudication phase
- [ ] POST /rules/:id/reviews with action='comment' returns 422
- [ ] Error toast indicates phase rejection
- [ ] Spec passes

title: Strengthen json_archive lifecycle test: assert exact timestamps preserved
description: `spec/services/import/json_archive_importer_spec.rb:570-578` asserts `triage_set_at`/`adjudicated_at` are `be_present` only. A bug that resets these to `Time.current` on import passes. Capture before-import timestamps; assert imported value `be_within(1.second).of(original)`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Capture original triage_set_at, adjudicated_at before export
- [ ] Assert imported values be_within(1.second).of(original)
- [ ] Spec catches a 'reset to Time.current on import' bug

title: Add site-admin move_to_rule test (User.admin=true, no project membership)
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb#PATCH /reviews/:id/move_to_rule` only tests project-admin and project-author. `authorize_admin_project` lets site-admins through (User.admin=true with no project membership). Add a context proving the IDOR guard pairs correctly with the site-admin escape hatch.

- [ ] Site admin successfully moves review across rules

title: Test parent-first walk in move_to_rule prevents validator failure
description: The parent-first walk in `move_review_subtree!` (reviews_controller.rb:618-624) is the whole point of the validator interaction — `responding_to_must_be_same_rule` reads parent.rule_id from DB via .pick. Children-first walk fails because child.rule_id moves before parent's does. No current test proves the ordering matters; the existing happy-path test passes even with children-first because there's only one reply.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test stubs Review#responses to yield reversed (children-first)
- [ ] Asserts validator raises + transaction rolls back
- [ ] Default ordering test (parent-first) succeeds
- [ ] Specs pass

Currently the only duplicate-payload assertion is on `canSave` (computed-property only).

title: Research: OrbStack + GlobalProtect VPN routing conflicts (MITRE dev env)
description:
**Context:** Extended debugging session trying to get local dev PostgreSQL working with OrbStack on a MITRE laptop with GlobalProtect VPN. Documents findings for future developers hitting the same wall.

## Findings

### OrbStack is closed-source
- Cannot inspect or patch the port forwarding proxy or DNS service
- https://github.com/orbstack/orbstack is issue tracker only, not source
- Only free for personal use, paid for commercial

### DNS cache bug (#2267 — open, no fix)
- When a container restarts, OrbStack's mDNS cache holds the OLD container IP
- `.orb.local` hostnames resolve to stale IPs even after container restart
- Workaround: assign static IPs in docker-compose (conflicts with multi-project dev)
- Enterprise users reported this since March 2026 — still open

### macOS 15 Local Network permission (#1452, #1620)
- Terminal apps need "Allow Local Network" in System Settings → Privacy & Security
- Without this, even correct OrbStack DNS fails with "no route to host"
- Common symptom: `nslookup` works but actual connection fails

### GlobalProtect VPN conflict
- GP dynamically adds routes for any 192.168.x.x subnet it sees traffic for
- OrbStack uses 192.168.x.x by default — GP captures and routes to VPN gateway
- Results in traffic to container IP going to MITRE corporate network instead of local bridge
- `netstat -rn | grep 192.168.167` shows the hijacked route via utun0

### Fix applied
- Configure OrbStack to use `198.19.x.x` subnet (not 192.168.x.x)
- OrbStack Settings → Docker → Engine:
  ```json
  {
    "tlscert": "~/.aws/mitre-ca-bundle.pem",
    "bip": "198.19.192.1/23",
    "default-address-pools": [
      {"base": "198.19.192.0/19", "size": 23},
      {"base": "198.19.224.0/20", "size": 23}
    ]
  }
  ```
- GlobalProtect does NOT claim 198.19.x.x (reserved for benchmarks RFC 2544)
- But GP will still claim any subnet OrbStack uses after restart → need to kill DNS cache

### Supplementary fix for scram-sha-256
- OrbStack's port forward proxy mangles PostgreSQL SCRAM-SHA-256 challenge-response
- Set `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: trust` in docker-compose.dev.yml
- Mastodon, GitLab do the same for dev/CI
- Production uses docker-compose.yml which does NOT set trust
- Documented with link to issue #2267 inline in the compose file

**Why:** Save the next developer 4 hours of debugging when they hit this.
**How to apply:** Reference this card when onboarding v2.x devs on MITRE-issued laptops.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved in this card for next dev hitting this
- [ ] OrbStack 198.19.x.x config snippet documented
- [ ] POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD trust rationale documented with issue link

title: Research: GitHub Actions supply chain hardening post tj-actions attack
description:
**Context:** Original release workflow (git-cliff + tag-triggered) was removed by will (commit 604d808) because GITHUB_TOKEN couldn't push CHANGELOG.md to branch-protected master. We researched the proper industry-standard fix.

## Findings

### tj-actions supply chain attack (March 2025)
- CVE-2025-30066: compromised `tj-actions/changed-files` impacted ~23,000 repos including Coinbase (70K customers)
- Attacker retroactively modified version tags to point to malicious commits
- Tags (`@v1`, `@v2`) are MUTABLE; SHAs are not
- OpenSSF guidance post-attack: pin ALL actions to full commit SHAs

### GitHub App token pattern (industry standard)
- GitLab, Semantic Release, and others use this approach
- Create a GitHub App scoped to the repo with `contents: write`
- Add app to branch protection "Allow specified actors to bypass required pull requests"
- Store `app-id` and `private-key` as secrets
- Use `actions/create-github-app-token@<SHA>` in workflow to mint a short-lived token
- App tokens are NOT tied to a human (PATs are) and are scoped to the specific repo

### Why this is better than PATs
- PATs require a human owner — attacker who compromises the human gets everything
- App tokens are scoped, short-lived, revocable
- GitHub auto-expires app tokens after 1 hour

### What NOT to do
- Never use `pull_request_target` trigger — runs with write permissions on fork PR code
- Never skip hooks (`--no-verify`) in CI
- Never use `@main` or `@master` refs for third-party actions

**Already tracked as:** vulcan-v3.x-8ij (separate card for implementing this)

**Why:** Document the security reasoning behind the planned release workflow restoration.
**How to apply:** Reference when implementing vulcan-v3.x-8ij.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved
- [ ] Referenced from release workflow card (vulcan-v3.x-8ij)

title: Research: OmniAuth provider lookup patterns, auto-link, nkf VM bug
description:
**Context:** Before implementing the auto-link feature we researched how major Rails apps handle OmniAuth provider conflicts and multi-provider linking.

## Findings

### GitLab
- Lookup by provider+uid FIRST, then email fallback
- `omniauth_auto_link_user` config setting (true / false / array of provider names)
- GitLab fork of omniauth-ldap was bumped to 2.3.0 (removes kconv dead require)
- Current GitLab: `gem 'gitlab_omniauth-ldap', '~> 2.3.0'`

### Discourse
- Uses separate `omniauth_identity` table (has_many :identities)
- Lookup always by (provider, uid) — never by email for security
- Auto-link is admin-approved, not auto

### Devise wiki
- Recommends the (provider, uid) → email fallback pattern
- Does NOT address the unauthenticated-user-with-matching-local-account case

### Vulcan's pick
- Single provider+uid on User model (not identity table — avoids migration)
- Global `VULCAN_AUTO_LINK_USER` setting (one ring)
- `email_verified` claim check for OIDC safety
- Human-readable error messages, clear UX

## nkf Ruby VM bug (#21967)
- Ruby 3.4+ VM crash in forked processes when nkf.so loads
- `gitlab_omniauth-ldap` 2.2.0 has dead `require 'kconv'` → loads nkf
- Fix: swap to `omniauth-ldap` 2.3.3 (original, no kconv), OR bump gitlab fork to 2.3.0
- We picked `omniauth-ldap` 2.3.3 because it's more actively maintained and has better thread-safety (option :mapping vs @@config)

**Links:**
- https://bugs.ruby-lang.org/issues/21967
- https://github.com/omniauth/omniauth-ldap
- GitLab docs on omniauth_auto_link_user

**Why:** Document the why behind the design decisions for future maintainers.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved in this card for future maintainers
- [ ] Referenced from epic description or PR body

title: Normalize PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS constant to array for consistency with siblings
description:
**Location:** `app/constants/project_member_constants.rb:9`

**Current code:**
```ruby
PROJECT_MEMBER_VIEWERS = %w[viewer reviewer author admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_AUTHORS = %w[author admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_REVIEWERS = %w[reviewer admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS = 'admin'   # <-- singular string, plural name
```

**Problem:** Siblings are arrays. `PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS` is a scalar string with a plural name. Used in `user.rb`:
```ruby
project.memberships.where(user_id: id, role: PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS).any?
```
Works because ActiveRecord accepts a scalar. But any future caller who assumes array semantics (`PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS.include?(role)`) gets String#include? which is substring match, not membership check. Footgun.

**Fix:** Normalize to an array: `PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS = %w[admin].freeze`. Update any references that assume a scalar.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed. Consider whether in-scope for this PR or follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Change PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS to %w[admin].freeze
- [ ] Audit all references and update if scalar was assumed
- [ ] user.rb can_admin_project? still works
- [ ] Membership specs still pass
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS is now %w[admin].freeze (array, not scalar).

title: Use falsy check in UsersTable typeColumn to handle undefined provider
description:
**Location:** `app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue:162-164`

**Current code:**
```js
typeColumn: function (user) {
  return user.provider === null ? "Local User" : user.provider.toUpperCase() + " User";
}
```

**Problem:** Strict equality with `null`. Rails `as_json` emits `null` for a nil column when the field is in `select`, so existing call sites are safe. But any future caller passing a user object WITHOUT the `provider` key (new endpoint, test fixture, v-bind with partial data) will hit `undefined.toUpperCase()` → TypeError. EditUserModal.vue's `providerLabel` uses `!user.provider ?` — the idiomatic pattern.

**Fix:** Use falsy check:
```js
typeColumn: (user) => !user.provider ? "Local User" : user.provider.toUpperCase() + " User"
```

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: null}) returns 'Local User'
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: undefined}) returns 'Local User' (does not throw)
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: 'oidc'}) returns 'OIDC User'
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({}) returns 'Local User' (no provider key)
- [ ] Replace === null with !user.provider
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. typeColumn uses falsy check, not strict === null.

title: Harden email_verified OIDC claim check against string 'false' from misconfigured providers
description:
**Location:** `app/models/user.rb:166`

**Current code:**
```ruby
if auth.info.respond_to?(:email_verified) && auth.info.email_verified == false
```

**Problem:** The comment above explicitly says "If the claim is absent, we trust the admin's decision. If explicitly false, refuse." Current code correctly lets `nil` pass (since `nil == false` is `false`). But providers that encode the field as string `"false"` (misconfigured OIDC attribute mappings, some SAML-to-OIDC bridges) would be treated as verified, bypassing the security check.

**Fix:** Use `ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(...)` — it returns `nil` for absent/nil, `true` for true/"true"/1, `false` for false/"false"/0.

```ruby
email_verified_claim = auth.info.respond_to?(:email_verified) ? auth.info.email_verified : nil
if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(email_verified_claim) == false
  # refuse
end
```

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=true → succeeds
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=false (boolean) → refused
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified='false' (string) → refused
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=nil → succeeds (backward compat)
- [ ] Test: auto-link without email_verified field → succeeds (backward compat)
- [ ] Use ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast for coercion
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Uses ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast for email_verified claim.

title: Log OmniAuth exception backtraces in all environments, not just development
description:
**Locations:**
- `app/models/user.rb:116-117` (from_omniauth rescue)
- `app/controllers/users/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb` (all omniauth_*_error handlers)

**Buggy pattern:**
```ruby
rescue StandardError => e
  Rails.logger.error "Failed to create/update user from OmniAuth: #{e.message}"
  Rails.logger.debug e.backtrace.join("\n") if Rails.env.development?
  raise
end
```

**Problem:** Production incidents lose the backtrace. Production operators who need to debug an OIDC failure CAN NOT get the stack trace even if they crank log level to debug — the line is gated on `Rails.env.development?`.

**Fix:** Drop the `if Rails.env.development?` gate. `Rails.logger.debug` is already conditionally emitted based on the current log level, so adding the backtrace to debug output is safe — ops can enable debug logging in production when investigating incidents.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Remove Rails.env.development? gate from all OmniAuth rescue handlers
- [ ] Verify backtrace logs at debug level in test env (Rails.logger.debug call happens)
- [ ] Same fix applied to user.rb:117 from_omniauth rescue
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed: removed Rails.env.development? gate from user.rb:116 backtrace logging. All envs now log backtraces at debug level. Test added.

title: Auto-compact automation: LLM-powered prepare/restore hooks
description:
Research and implement automated prepare-compact/restore-context for auto-compact.

## Problem
When auto-compact triggers, our /prepare-compact and /restore-context skills can't run automatically because hooks only support bash scripts for PreCompact and SessionStart events. Hook types "prompt" and "agent" (which support LLM reasoning) are NOT available for these events.

## Research Findings (Session 2026-02-16)
- GitHub issues: #14258 (29 reactions), #3537 (17 reactions), #17237 (7 reactions) — all open, no Anthropic response
- Hook type "agent" exists but NOT supported for PreCompact/SessionStart
- PostCompact hook does NOT exist (most requested feature)
- SessionStart:compact stdout injection is buggy (#15174)

## Best Workaround: mvara-ai/precompact-hook pattern
- PreCompact bash hook spawns `claude -p` subprocess with transcript
- Fresh Claude instance generates structured recovery brief
- Writes to file, no stdout injection needed
- See: github.com/mvara-ai/precompact-hook

## Other Tools Reviewed
- ContextRecoveryHook (claudefa.st) — script-based, no LLM
- claude-mem (thedotmack) — SQLite memory with LLM compression
- hookshot (CorridorSecurity) — Go typed structs for hooks
- Custom /compact instructions pattern (EmanuelFaria, #13572)

## Implementation Plan
1. Enhance PreCompact hook: capture state + spawn `claude -p` for strategic context
2. Enhance SessionStart:compact hook: inject recovery context + CLAUDE.md instructions
3. Test with auto-compact enabled
4. Consider contributing to #14258 with our findings

## Key Files
- ~/.claude/hooks/pre-compact-save-state.sh (existing)
- ~/.claude/hooks/post-compact-restore-state.sh (existing)
- Skills: /prepare-compact, /restore-context

title: v2.3.0 MIGRATION: Apply SRG hierarchy learnings from v2.2.x
description:
# SRG Hierarchy and ID Field Mapping

## Three-Tier Hierarchy

1. **CORE SRGs** (3 foundational documents)
   - Application Core SRG
   - Operating System Core SRG  
   - Network Core SRG
   
2. **SRGs** (product/platform-specific, derived from Core)
   - Each requirement references a Core SRG requirement via srg_id
   - Example: SRG-APP-000507 (from Core Application SRG)
   
3. **STIGs or Components** (implementation guides)
   - Each rule references an SRG requirement via srg_id

## Data Flow

CORE SRG → SRG → STIG
CORE SRG → SRG → Component

## Field Mapping in RuleOverview

### When viewing a STIG:
- **Rule ID**: SV-12345r1 (STIG rule identifier, version embedded)
- **Satisfies (SRG)**: SRG-OS-000001 (which SRG requirement this implements)

### When viewing an SRG:
- **Requirement ID**: [SRG requirement ID]
- **Core SRG**: SRG-APP-000507 (which Core SRG requirement this derives from)

## Important Notes

- Rule/Requirement "version" (r1, r2) is XCCDF metadata embedded in rule_id
- Separate version field is redundant and removed from UI
- srg_id field means different things in different contexts:
  - In STIG: Points UP to SRG requirement
  - In SRG: Points UP to Core SRG requirement
  
## Future Work

- Core SRGs may be added to Vulcan database for completeness
- Would enable tracing full lineage: Core → SRG → Implementation
- Currently Core SRGs are external reference only

Document valid_password? hidden rehash side-effect at unlink call site
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:95` (in `unlink_identity` action)

**Issue:**
```ruby
unless user.valid_password?(params[:current_password].to_s)
```

**Hidden side effect:** `User#valid_password?` is overridden in user.rb:56 to do bcrypt→PBKDF2 rehashing on successful login:
```ruby
def valid_password?(password)
  if encrypted_password.start_with?('$2a$', '$2b$')
    # ... verify with BCrypt ...
    update_columns(encrypted_password: new_hash, password_salt: new_salt) if result
  else
    super
  end
end
```

So calling `valid_password?` in `unlink_identity` has a hidden write side-effect when the user still has a legacy bcrypt password. Not a bug today — the rehash is idempotent — but:
1. Unlink would fail silently on a read-only DB replica
2. The write happens during what looks like a read/verify step
3. A future refactor might break the assumption

**Fix:** Add a code comment at the unlink call site explicitly referencing `User#valid_password?` override and its migration write. No code change needed today, but document the dependency.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed. Documentation-only card.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Add comment at registrations_controller unlink_identity password check
- [ ] Reference User#valid_password? bcrypt→PBKDF2 migration in the comment
- [ ] No code change required
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Comment documenting bcrypt→PBKDF2 rehash side-effect added at unlink call site.

value: DISA inheritance pattern (Vendor STIG Process Guide v4r1 §4.1.15): a requirement fully mitigated by another STIG is encoded as Status='Applicable - Does Not Meet' + Mitigation field carrying canonical 'This requirement is fully mitigated by [parent-STIG-RuleID]' sentence + Status Justification per §4.1.17. There is NO 'Inherited' status. Vulcan today uses satisfied_by → vendor_comments + auto-overrides status to Configurable, which is non-DISA-canonical. Fix is plan task 31 (deferred follow-up).

PR #717 vocabulary-layering: DB columns + API + CSV/OSCAL export use DISA-native (concur/non_concur/adjudicated/check_content/etc.); UI templates + error msgs use friendly English (Accept/Decline/Closed). Two source-of-truth files: en.yml + triageVocabulary.js; pre-commit greps catch drift

Vulcan ComponentBlueprint gotcha: do NOT put fields in DEFAULT (top-level) fields list if Project#available_components.select(...) doesn't include them. The :index view inherits default fields and is consumed by ProjectBlueprint :show for available_components — a column-limited query. Putting comment_phase / comment_period_* in default broke /projects/:id with ActiveModel::MissingAttributeError. Fix: put schema fields in :show + :editor views only, NOT default. Regression test in spec/requests/projects_show_spec.rb.

Rails 7+ YAML safe-dump rejects ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone unless allowlisted. Required for the audited gem to serialize datetime attribute changes (e.g. Component#comment_period_ends_at). Allowlist in config/application.rb via config.active_record.yaml_column_permitted_classes = [Symbol, Date, Time, BigDecimal, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone, ActiveSupport::TimeZone, ActiveSupport::HashWithIndifferentAccess]. REQUIRES Rails server restart to take effect (config/application.rb is boot-time). Without it, audit-emitting save raises Psych::DisallowedClass which the catch-all StandardError rescue turns into a 302/500.

PR #717 design (Aaron 2026-04-29, refined 2026-04-30 after 3-agent plan review): Vulcan comments are first-class objects, not signals auto-inherited through the satisfies graph. Comment data stays on the rule it was posted on (rule_id stable). Triage decisions live with the comment. Counts are local-only. Cross-rule consolidation is EXPLICIT via author/admin operations, not implicit via graph traversal. Operations: mark-as-duplicate (schema: triage_status='duplicate' + duplicate_of_review_id, validators ALREADY exist in review.rb at lines 96-102, only the picker UI is new). Cross-rule discoverability is INFORMATIONAL via the EXISTING Satisfies panel (small enhancement to show comment counts — NOT a new component). Rejected: read-side query union of comments along satisfies. PR-717 plan tasks IN scope: 23 (counts on Satisfies panel), 24 (mark-as-duplicate UI + chained-dup validator), 27 (smoke + Task 99 update). DEFERRED to follow-up phase: 25 (admin force-withdraw UI), 26 (admin move-to-rule UI). For this phase, admin force-withdraw + move-to-rule + hard-delete are documented as Rails-console procedures in docs/runbook-public-comment-admin-actions.md — auditable via VulcanAuditable + audit_comment setter. Federal-compliance note: vulcan_audited only: list does NOT include rule_id today (review.rb:38) — when Task 26 ships in a follow-up phase, add rule_id to the audit list.

Vulcan PR #717 disposition matrix output: CSV is in scope (Task 29), OSCAL is DEFERRED with documented dead-end reasoning. There is NO canonical OSCAL model for public-comment review disposition. SAR's <observation>/<finding> looks closest but is designed for control-pass/fail assessment, not commentary on draft document wording — the semantic stretch is real. Custom <prop name='...' ns='urn:disa:public-comment-review:...'> extensions defeat OSCAL interop. DISA today consumes CSV/Excel for disposition. If structured machine-readable output needed later: published JSON schema under SAF namespace OR DISA-supplied OSCAL profile when one exists — NOT a private SAR mapping. When Aaron asks 'what are you doing' or 'why did you X', he wants TRANSPARENCY while you keep going — not a halt. Answer briefly in the next response, but DO NOT stop the in-flight work mid-stream. Pausing to over-explain destroys momentum and makes him think you're lost. The right pattern: continue the click sequence / TDD loop, summarize at the natural breakpoint.

Vulcan v2.x esbuild packs each have their own axios singleton (bundle isolation). FormMixin in one pack does NOT set X-CSRF-Token globally for other packs. Any modal/page that uses axios.patch/post/put MUST include FormMixin directly. Vulcan UX rule (Aaron 2026-04-29): when a feature or control is unavailable, render it visibly DISABLED with an explanatory tooltip — never hide it via v-if. Applies app-wide (lock toggles, comment icons, action buttons, dropdowns, etc.). Reason: hidden features are non-discoverable; disabled-with-tooltip teaches the user what's available and what enables it. Concrete pattern: SectionCommentIcon shows greyed icon + tooltip 'Set the rule status before commenting' when status=NYD; same icon + tooltip 'Rule is locked — comments closed' when locked. Don't reach for v-if to remove a control — use :disabled + a tooltip + a CSS inactive class.

Don't use "federal compliance" / "federal-compliance" as a magic justification phrase in code, comments, commit messages, or plan files. It reads as faked authority without a citation (same pattern as the fabricated NIST 800-53 BOM claim). Audit / log / review / deliverable language is fine — drop only the "federal" qualifier. Aaron 2026-05-01: "It makes us look like we don't know what we are talking about." 14 references swept clean in commit 013cd2b.

Aaron 2026-05-01 (PR-717 review remediation, .8 zip-modify incident): agent review reports are SIGNALS, not gospel. They frame issues from a single angle and may encode wrong requirements. NEVER implement an agent's suggested fix shape without (1) verifying the framing matches actual user intent, (2) researching the standard/best-practice for the actual problem, (3) reading the project's existing patterns for similar work. The pattern that gets me in trouble: 'agent says do X, here's what X looks like in code' — instead of 'agent says X is a problem; let me research what X really means and what the proper solution is in this codebase'. Specific failures this session: (a) .6 framed a policy decision as a security gap because the security agent labeled it that way, (b) .3 used update_columns to bypass Devise validation in fixtures instead of researching the right Rails pattern (likely save(validate: false) or FactoryBot trait), (c) .8 about to modify a zip's JSON to bypass a destroy! callback error instead of debugging the let_it_be in-memory-cache interaction. Rule: research → docs → codebase patterns → THEN propose, never barrel forward. NO GUESSING.

Audited gem auto-populates request_uuid per Rails request via Rack middleware. ALL audits in one HTTP call share UUID. Wrapped via AuditEventBundle PORO + VulcanAudit.bundled_with(audit_id). NULL outside HTTP — see card 14r for backfill.

DISA STIG public/CUI split (Vendor STIG Process v4r1 §6): only Applicable-Configurable rules go public on Cyber Exchange. NA / IM / DNM rules are Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI), made available only to Authorizing Officials. Means: an inherited-from-parent rule (DNM+mitigation) NEVER appears in the published STIG. PR-717 commenter UI may surface this as a 'Will publish' / 'CUI only' badge per rule (deferred to plan task 32).

NEVER claim Vulcan does/doesn't do X without reading the actual code that handles X. Aaron built this app — every fabricated claim about behavior gets caught instantly. When mapping external specs (DISA PDFs, OSCAL, etc.) to 'gaps' in Vulcan, READ the relevant source files FIRST. If unsure: say 'I don't know — I'd need to check the code' rather than asserting a gap.

Vulcan rule editor UX gap: when a rule is satisfied_by another (parent), the child rule today shows row_editable?=false in the spreadsheet view (rule.rb:329-335) but the rule editor itself does not visibly lock or semi-lock the child's content. The semantic of satisfies is "covered by parent — edit there." UI should make this unambiguous: either lock the child editor entirely with a clear pointer to the parent, or semi-lock with a banner explaining the inheritance and offering a one-click jump to the parent. Today's UX leaves users editing children that should be edited via the parent. Follow-up rule-editor UX work; not in PR-717 scope.
v3 architecture layers: API (apis/*.api.ts) → Store (stores/*.store.ts, Pinia) → Composable (composables/useXxx.ts) → Page (pages/**/*.vue) → Component. Test each layer independently.

Vulcan project-removal policy (Aaron 2026-05-01 on PR-717 .6): if a user is removed from a project, they have no remaining authority on anything project-scoped — including their own pending comments (cannot withdraw, cannot edit). The comment ITSELF stays put (project record stability); the user just loses ability to alter it. withdraw is soft terminal state (triage_status=withdrawn), update is text-only with audit trail — nothing is deleted by removal under any path. Implemented via authorize_viewer_project on withdraw+update in reviews_controller.rb. Pattern applies broadly: any project-scoped action should be gated by current membership, not just historical authorship/ownership.

Vulcan cascade ownership: Rails dependent: :destroy + Postgres FK on_delete: :restrict (safety net). on_delete: :cascade skips Rails callbacks → loses audited per-row destroy events. PR-717 .4 commit 33b2bea.

Vulcan Review#move_to_rule (Task 26 deferred; runbook §2): walk PARENT-FIRST when reassigning rule_id to a thread. The responding_to_must_be_same_rule validator (review.rb:282) reads parent.rule_id from the DB via .pick — children-first walk fails because child.rule_id moves before parent's does. Parent-first works because the parent's own responding_to_review_id is nil so validator short-circuits at line 278; descendants then see parent already at target. Same pattern applies if/when promoted to UI. Also note: vulcan_audited only: list (review.rb:38) does NOT include :rule_id today — move-to-rule produces no audit record without that column being added first.

Vulcan pre-destroy snapshots use ISO8601 strings for timestamps (not Time objects). Avoids Rails 7.1+ YAML safe-load break on Audit#find. Pattern: val.respond_to?(:iso8601) ? val.iso8601 : val in Review#snapshot_attributes. PR-717 .4 commit d3314da.
